title: Perforce Enabling Innovation Without Introducing Risk at DAC 2026 - Semiwiki
gist: Perforce DAC 2026 promo positions its P4 version control, IPLM (IP lifecycle management), and VersIC analog design editor as AI-governance infrastructure for chip design teams, explicitly contrasting Git's rewritable history with P4's immutable change lineage as the system of record needed for AI-agent-driven workflows.

themes.theme: Immutable version control as a requirement for AI-agent workflows

insights.claim: Perforce explicitly contrasts its P4 product against Git, arguing that Git lets teams rewrite history while P4 provides an immutable lineage of changes for users and their AI agents, positioning this as a necessary system-of-record for AI-driven chip design workflows.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Frames immutable version history as a governance requirement specifically created by AI-agent adoption in engineering workflows — a competitive angle Perforce is using against Git-based tooling as AI-assisted/agentic design scales, relevant to enterprise DevOps tooling vendors' positioning in semiconductor design.
insights.quote: Git lets teams re-write history. P4 provides an immutable lineage of changes for your users and their agents.

insights.claim: Perforce's IPLM (IP Lifecycle Management) product now includes an MCP-based (Model Context Protocol) AI Assistant delivered through a 'Perforce Agentic Gateway,' alongside Neo4j 5 graph-database support, aimed at giving AI-driven workflows a centralized, traceable system of record for IP reuse across a company's SoC-to-company-level IP library.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Confirms Perforce is building explicit MCP/agentic-AI integration into its IP lifecycle management product, a concrete signal of how enterprise chip-design tooling vendors are adapting to agentic AI adoption rather than just chip-design EDA vendors themselves.
insights.quote: IPLM MCP through the Perforce Agentic Gateway
